hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" | grep -q -- '--no-verify\|--no-gpg-sign'; then echo '--no-verify is blocked by policy' >&2; exit 2; fi

hooks.prompt: Analyze this bash command. Is it a destructive git operation (reset --hard, push --force, clean -f, branch -D, checkout . on modified files)? If destructive, explain why it should be blocked. If safe, say it is safe to proceed.

hooks.command: FILE="$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS"; if [[ -z "$FILE" ]]; then exit 0; fi; if [[ "$FILE" =~ \.(test|spec)\. ]] || [[ "$FILE" =~ __tests__/ ]] || [[ "$FILE" =~ /tests?/ ]] || [[ "$FILE" =~ \.env\.example$ ]] || [[ "$FILE" =~ \.(md|json|yaml|yml|css|scss|html|txt|svg|lock)$ ]] || [[ "$FILE" =~ \.(d\.ts)$ ]]; then exit 0; fi; CONTENT="$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT"; PATTERNS='AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}|ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}|gho_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}|xoxb-[0-9]{10,}|xoxp-[0-9]{10,}|glpat-[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{20}'; if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE "$PATTERNS"; then echo 'BLOCKED: Suspected credential/secret detected in file content' >&2; exit 2; fi

hooks.prompt: Check if the file being edited is a source file (not a test, config, type definition, documentation, markdown, JSON, YAML, CSS, or lock file). If it is a source file (e.g., .ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx, .py, .go, .rs, .rb), check whether a corresponding test file exists in common test locations (__tests__/, *.test.*, *.spec.*, tests/, *_test.*). If no test file exists for this source file, block the edit — tests must be written first (TDD). Otherwise allow it.

hooks.command: if [[ "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" =~ \.(js|jsx|ts|tsx|json|css|scss|html|md|yaml|yml)$ ]]; then npx --no-install prettier --write "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" 2>/dev/null || true; fi

if [[ "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" =~ \.(js|jsx|ts|tsx)$ ]]; then npx --no-install eslint --fix "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" 2>/dev/null; if ! npx --no-install eslint "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" 2>&1; then echo 'ESLint errors remain after auto-fix' >&2; exit 2; fi; fi
if [[ "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" =~ \.(ts|tsx)$ ]]; then npx --no-install tsc --noEmit 2>&1 || { echo 'TypeScript type errors detected' >&2; exit 2; }; fi

FILE="$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS"; if [[ -z "$FILE" ]] || [[ "$FILE" =~ \.(test|spec)\. ]] || [[ "$FILE" =~ __tests__/ ]] || [[ ! "$FILE" =~ \.(js|jsx|ts|tsx|py|go|rs|rb)$ ]]; then exit 0; fi; DIR=$(dirname "$FILE"); BASE=$(basename "$FILE" | sed 's/\.[^.]*$//'); if [[ -f "${DIR}/${BASE}.test.ts" ]] || [[ -f "${DIR}/${BASE}.test.tsx" ]] || [[ -f "${DIR}/${BASE}.test.js" ]] || [[ -f "${DIR}/${BASE}.spec.ts" ]] || [[ -f "${DIR}/${BASE}.spec.tsx" ]] || [[ -f "${DIR}/${BASE}.spec.js" ]] || [[ -f "${DIR}/__tests__/${BASE}.test.ts" ]] || [[ -f "${DIR}/__tests__/${BASE}.test.tsx" ]] || [[ -f "${DIR}/__tests__/${BASE}.test.js" ]]; then TESTFILE=$(ls "${DIR}/${BASE}".{test,spec}.{ts,tsx,js} "${DIR}/__tests__/${BASE}".test.{ts,tsx,js} 2>/dev/null | head -1); if [[ -n "$TESTFILE" ]]; then if [[ -f package.json ]] || [[ -f ../package.json ]]; then npx --no-install jest --passWithNoTests "$TESTFILE" 2>/dev/null || npx --no-install vitest run "$TESTFILE" 2>/dev/null || true; elif [[ "$FILE" =~ \.py$ ]]; then python -m pytest "$TESTFILE" 2>/dev/null || true; elif [[ "$FILE" =~ \.go$ ]]; then go test "$TESTFILE" 2>/dev/null || true; fi; fi; fi

hooks.prompt: Context is about to be compacted. Summarize the current session: what the user is working on, progress made, key decisions, open items, and next steps. This summary will be preserved through compaction.

Review this conversation. Compare what the user originally requested with what was actually implemented. Were all requirements addressed? Were any unrequested changes made? Report any gaps or confirm completion.
